contraindications.clinicalRationale: NSAIDs are nephrotoxic drugs that can cause acute creatinine rise and contribute to acute kidney injury, particularly when used concurrently with ACE inhibitors or ARBs in patients with CKD
contraindications.riskDescription: Acute kidney injury and >30% increase in serum creatinine
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Discontinue NSAIDs and consider alternative pain management strategies
contraindications.sourceSnippet: reassess concomitant medications (e.g., diuretics, NSAIDs), consider renal artery stenosis... concurrent use of nephrotoxic drugs (e.g., NSAIDs)

conditionDescriptions: Hyperkalemia with ACE inhibitor or ARB therapy
contraindications.clinicalRationale: NSAIDs can impair kidney excretion of potassium and exacerbate hyperkalemia in patients receiving ACE inhibitors or ARBs
contraindications.riskDescription: Worsening hyperkalemia and impaired potassium excretion
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Discontinue over-the-counter NSAIDs and consider alternative pain management
contraindications.sourceSnippet: History of the use of over-the-counter nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), supplements, and herbal treatments should be pursued, and patients should be counseled to discontinue these remedies if present.
